task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is visible on the screen.
The OpenToonz interface shows 'dwanko_run.tnz' loaded, typically visible in the application title bar, the project browser, or the xsheet/timeline.

task_completion.description: Set the render output format to MP4
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog in OpenToonz is open and the file format dropdown or selection is explicitly set to 'mp4'.

task_completion.description: Set the output path to the required directory and filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the save in/folder path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/outputs/' (or similar relative path) and the file name set to 'rendered_animation'.

task_completion.description: Render the animation to the output file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar/window is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager terminal shows 'rendered_animation.mp4' successfully created in the target folder with a file size of at least 10 KB.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz graphical interface (menus, output settings, render buttons) rather than relying solely on terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The animation was genuinely rendered by the software
integrity.visual_evidence: There is evidence of the OpenToonz render process (e.g., a render progress popup or frame-by-frame processing) rather than the agent simply renaming an existing MP4 file.

integrity.description: The agent did not create a fake empty file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not use commands like 'touch /home/ga/OpenToonz/outputs/rendered_animation.mp4' in the terminal to create an empty file to trick the verifier.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected scene file is 'dwanko_run.tnz'. The expected output path is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/outputs/rendered_animation.mp4'. A successful render must produce a file of at least 10 KB, which indicates a genuine video file was generated rather than an empty placeholder.
